devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । षष्ठः स्कन्धःपञ्चदशोऽध्यायः । चित्रकेतवेऽङ्गिरोनारदयोरुपदेशःश्रीशुक उवाचऊचतुर्मृतकोपान्ते पतितं मृतकोपमम् । शोका...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇa ṣaṣṭhaḥ skandhaḥpañcadaśo citraketave śokābhibhūtaṃ rājānaṃ bodhayantau saduktibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ budh ], [vocative dual from √ budh ], [accusative dual from √ budh ]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Bringing back to consciousness the king, with their soft and wise words, the sages Aṅgiras and Nārada spoke to the king who was overpowered with grief, and was lying like a dead person by the side of the dead child.

devanagari: कोऽयं स्यात् तव राजेन्द्र भवान् यमनुशोचति । त्वं चास्य कतमः सृष्टौ पुरेदानीमतः परम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ko tvaṃ cāsya katamaḥ sṛṣṭau puredānīmataḥ param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: “What can be your relationship, in the previous and the present birth, Oh great King, with the child whom you are mourning? How was he related to you in the previous birth, the present birth and will be related in the future one?

devanagari: यथा प्रयान्ति संयान्ति स्रोतोवेगेन वालुकाः । संयुज्यन्ते वियुज्यन्ते तथा कालेन देहिनः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā prayānti saṃyānti srotovegena vālukāḥ saṃyujyante viyujyante tathā kālena dehinaḥ
translation: Just as the grains of sand come together and separate by the force of the stream of water, so are embodied beings brought together and separated by Time.

devanagari: यथा धानासु वै धाना भवन्ति न भवन्ति च । एवं भूतेषु भूतानि चोदितान् ईशमायया ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā dhānāsu vai dhānā bhavanti na bhavanti ca evaṃ bhūteṣu bhūtāni coditān īśamāyayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Just as seeds may or may not produce corn, similarly Bhūtas prompted by God’s Māyā may or may not produce Bhūtas.

devanagari: वयं च त्वं च ये चेमे तुल्यकालाश्चराचराः । जन्ममृत्योर्यथा पश्चात् प्राङ्नैवमधुनापि भोः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ ca tvaṃ ca ye ceme tulyakālāścarācarāḥ janmamṛtyoryathā paścāt prāṅnaivamadhunāpi bhoḥ
translation: We, you, the mobile and immobile creatures which are contemporaneous with us, were not so before the present birth nor are so in the present birth, nor shall be so after death. (Due to the nonexistence of these before and after, they are unsubstantial or unreal like dreams).

devanagari: भूतैर्भूतानि भूतेशः सृजत्यवति हन्त्यजः । आत्मसृष्टैरस्वतन्त्रैः अनपेक्षोऽपि बालवत् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtairbhūtāni bhūteśaḥ sṛjatyavati hantyajaḥ ātmasṛṣṭairasvatantraiḥ anapekṣo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [locative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: The Lord of Bhūtas who is himself unborn (and therefore imperishable) and has no purpose to achieve, like a playful boy, creates, protects and destroys the Bhūtas by means of Bhūtas which are created by him and hence dependent on him.

devanagari: देहेन देहिनो राजन् देहाद् देहोऽभिजायते । बीजादेव यथा बीजं देह्यर्थ इव शाश्वतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dehena dehino rājan dehād deho bījādeva yathā bījaṃ dehyartha iva śāśvataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Oh King, the body of a son is born from the body (of the mother) through another body (namely that of the father) just as a seed sprouts forth from another seed. But the occupant of the body is eternal like earth and other principles.

devanagari: देहदेहिविभागोऽयं अविवेककृतः पुरा । जातिव्यक्तिविभागोऽयं यथा वस्तुनि कल्पितः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: dehadehivibhāgo jātivyaktivibhāgo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation:
It is from eternity that this (above-mentioned) distinction between a body and the occupant of the body (the Soul) has been presumed through ignorance, as the distinction between the class or the generic property (jāti) and the individuality of a thing (vyakti) is conceived as existing in the thing itself (such distinction between interdependants like jāti and vyakti are untenable).
[Note: After verse No. 8, the text before Padaratnāvalī adds four verses:]
(1) Just as the difference or changes due to fuel are wrongly attributed to the fire, similarly the conditions affecting the body, viz., difference, birth, decline, growth and destruction are wrongly attributed to the Jīva who is distinct from the body. This misconception is due to Karma.
(2) It is due to Jīva’s intimate relation to the body and the false identification of the body with the Soul, that the above-mentioned conditions (of the body) appear to be the conditions of the Jīva as in a dream. As everything else is a source of fear, one should not ponder over anything leading to evil consequences.
(3) Just as the appearance of a serpent or a tiger or another (frightening) object like it, in a dream, causes fear, as the dream appearance is identified with the reality in a wakeful condition, so is the Saṃsāra due to the misconception grown since eternity.
(4) The idea of the world of sons (or relatives) and other possessions depends on the mind. It is due to the influence of the Ego which creates likes and dislikes in the mind and leads to merits and demerits. One should clear away the ignorance in the form of the notions like me and mine, and fix one’s mind on the all-powerful Omnipresent Lord Vāsudeva.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचएवं आश्वासितो राजा चित्रकेतुर्द्विजोक्तिभिः । विमृज्य पाणिना वक्त्रं आधिम्लानमभाषत ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevaṃ āśvāsito rājā citraketurdvijoktibhiḥ vimṛjya pāṇinā vaktraṃ ādhimlānamabhāṣata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When king Citraketu was thus consoled by the words of the Brāhmaṇa sages, he wiped out with his hand, his face which was withered through grief for his son, and addressed them thus.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाचकौ युवां ज्ञानसम्पन्नौ महिष्ठौ च महीयसाम् । अवधूतेन वेषेण गूढौ इह समागतौ ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovācakau yuvāṃ jñānasampannau mahiṣṭhau ca mahīyasām avadhūtena veṣeṇa gūḍhau iha samāgatau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ guh class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ guh class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ guh class 1 verb]

translation:
The king (Citraketu) said:
Who are you both so full of spiritual knowledge, the most worshipful ones among the adorables who have come here disguised as Avadhūtas?

devanagari: चरन्ति ह्यवनौ कामं ब्राह्मणा भगवत्प्रियाः । मादृशां ग्राम्यबुद्धीनां बोधायोन्मत्तलिङ्गिनः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: caranti hyavanau kāmaṃ brāhmaṇā bhagavatpriyāḥ mādṛśāṃ grāmyabuddhīnāṃ bodhāyonmattaliṅginaḥ
translation: For, persons who have realized Brahman and who are dear to the glorious Lord, range over the world in the guise of mad persons, for guiding and counselling sensually- minded people like me.

devanagari: कुमारो नारद ऋभुः अङ्गिरा देवलोऽसितः । अपान्तरतमो व्यासो मार्कण्डेयोऽथ गौतमः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kumāro nārada ṛbhuḥ aṅgirā devalo apāntaratamo vyāso mārkaṇḍeyo
translation: (For example) Sanatkumāra, Nārada, Ṛbhu, Aṅgiras, Devala, Amita, Apāntarātma, Vyāsa, Mārkeṇḍeya [Mārkaṇḍeya?] and Gautama.

devanagari: वसिष्ठो भगवान् रामः कपिलो बादरायणिः । दुर्वासा याज्ञवल्क्यश्च जातुकर्णस्तथाऽरुणिः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: vasiṣṭho bhagavān rāmaḥ kapilo bādarāyaṇiḥ durvāsā yājñavalkyaśca jātukarṇastathā
translation: Vasiṣṭha, the glorious Paraśurāma, Kapila, Bādarayaṇi (Śuka), Durvāsas, Yājñavalkya, Jātūkarṇya and Āruṇi.

devanagari: रोमशश्च्यवनो दत्त आसुरिः सपतञ्जलिः । ऋषिर्वेदशिरा बोध्यो मुनिः पञ्चशिखस्तथा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: romaśaścyavano datta āsuriḥ sapatañjaliḥ ṛṣirvedaśirā bodhyo muniḥ pañcaśikhastathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sap class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sap class 1 verb]

translation: Romaśa, Cyavana, Datta, Patañjali, Āsuri, the sage Vedaśiras, the sage Bodhya, and Pañcaśiras.

devanagari: हिरण्यनाभः कौसल्यः श्रुतदेव ऋतध्वजः । एते परे च सिद्धेशाः चरन्ति ज्ञानहेतवः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: hiraṇyanābhaḥ kausalyaḥ śrutadeva ṛtadhvajaḥ ete pare ca siddheśāḥ caranti jñānahetavaḥ
translation: Hiraṇyanābha, Kausalya, Śrutadeva, Ṛtadhvaja—these and other masters of spiritual powers go about in the world with the object of imparting spiritual knowledge.

devanagari: तस्माद् युवां ग्राम्यपशोः मम मूढधियः प्रभू । अन्धे तमसि मग्नस्य ज्ञानदीप उदीर्यताम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmād yuvāṃ grāmyapaśoḥ mama mūḍhadhiyaḥ prabhū andhe tamasi magnasya jñānadīpa udīryatām
translation: Therefore, you both are the masters of a stupid and ignorant brute like me. Enlighten me with the lamp of your knowledge—me who am plunged into the blinding darkness of ignorance.

devanagari: श्रीअङ्गिरा उवाचअहं ते पुत्रकामस्य पुत्रदोऽस्म्यङ्गिरा नृप । एष ब्रह्मसुतः साक्षात् नारदो भगवान् ऋषिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīaṅgirā uvācaahaṃ te putrakāmasya putrado eṣa brahmasutaḥ sākṣāt nārado bhagavān ṛṣiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Aṅgiras said:
Oh King, I am Aṅgiras who blessed you with a son as you longed for one. This is the veritable son of Brahmā, the worshipful divine sage Nārada.

devanagari: इत्थं त्वां पुत्रशोकेन मग्नं तमसि दुस्तरे । अतदर्हमनुस्मृत्य महापुरुषगोचरम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ tvāṃ putraśokena magnaṃ tamasi dustare atadarhamanusmṛtya mahāpuruṣagocaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ majj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ majj class 6 verb]

translation: Finding you, a devotee of the Supreme Person, plunged in the deep impassable darkness of grief for the son, though you are not fit to be so affected, we have arrived here to help you out of it, with our grace. For, you are dear to Brāhmaṇas and votary of the Lord and thus do not deserve to be so despondent.

devanagari: अनुग्रहाय भवतः प्राप्तौ आवां इह प्रभो । ब्रह्मण्यो भगवद्‍भक्तो नावसीदितुमर्हसि ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: anugrahāya bhavataḥ prāptau āvāṃ iha prabho brahmaṇyo bhagavad‍bhakto nāvasīditumarhasi
translation: Finding you, a devotee of the Supreme Person, plunged in the deep impassable darkness of grief for the son, though you are not fit to be so affected, we have arrived here to help you out of it, with our grace. For, you are dear to Brāhmaṇas and votary of the Lord and thus do not deserve to be so despondent.

devanagari: तदैव ते परं ज्ञानं ददामि गृहमागतः । ज्ञात्वान्याभिनिवेशं ते पुत्रमेव ददावहम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tadaiva te paraṃ jñānaṃ dadāmi gṛhamāgataḥ jñātvānyābhiniveśaṃ te putrameva dadāvaham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active first dual], [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active first dual], [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When I visited your house, at that very time (of our first meeting), I would have imparted to you spiritual knowledge. But knowing your insistence and longing for something else (viz, the birth of a son), I blessed you with a son.

devanagari: अधुना पुत्रिणां तापो भवतैवानुभूयते । एवं दारा गृहा रायो विविधैश्वर्यसम्पदः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: adhunā putriṇāṃ tāpo bhavataivānubhūyate evaṃ dārā gṛhā rāyo vividhaiśvaryasampadaḥ
translation: Now, you experience the agony suffered by persons having sons. In the same way (are the agonies of persons who have) wives, houses, wealth, power and prosperity of various kinds, objects of enjoyment like sound etc., fleeting powers of sovereignty, the earth, kingdom, the army, the treasury, servants, ministers, friends and relations.

devanagari: शब्दादयश्च विषयाः चला राज्यविभूतयः । मही राज्यं बलं कोशो भृत्यामात्याः सुहृज्जनाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śabdādayaśca viṣayāḥ calā rājyavibhūtayaḥ mahī rājyaṃ balaṃ kośo bhṛtyāmātyāḥ suhṛjjanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj ]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ rāj ], [accusative single from √ rāj ]

translation: Now, you experience the agony suffered by persons having sons. In the same way (are the agonies of persons who have) wives, houses, wealth, power and prosperity of various kinds, objects of enjoyment like sound etc., fleeting powers of sovereignty, the earth, kingdom, the army, the treasury, servants, ministers, friends and relations.

devanagari: सर्वेऽपि शूरसेनेमे शोकमोहभयार्तिदाः । गन्धर्वनगरप्रख्याः स्वप्नमायामनोरथाः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarve gandharvanagaraprakhyāḥ svapnamāyāmanorathāḥ
translation: Oh King Śūrasena, all these are the sources of grief, delusion, fear and affliction, for they are momentary like (the proverbial) city of Gandharvas in the sky, and unreal like a dream, delusion and fancies.

devanagari: दृश्यमाना विनार्थेन न दृश्यन्ते मनोभवाः । कर्मभिर्ध्यायतो नाना कर्माणि मनसोऽभवन् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛśyamānā vinārthena na dṛśyante manobhavāḥ karmabhirdhyāyato nānā karmāṇi manaso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ dṛś class 1 verb]

translation: They are mental images, for they are perceived without being real or substantial and disappear the next moment. For a man brooding over objects through the force of tendencies of actions (karma) in the previous birth, various activities proceed from the mind.

devanagari: अयं हि देहिनो देहो द्रव्यज्ञानक्रियात्मकः । देहिनो विविधक्लेश सन्तापकृदुदाहृतः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ hi dehino deho dravyajñānakriyātmakaḥ dehino vividhakleśa santāpakṛdudāhṛtaḥ
translation: In fact, this body of embodied beings is constituted of gross elements, conative and cognitive senses. It is said, by the knowers of the reality, to be the cause of various afflictions and torments to the Jīva who identifies itself with its body.

devanagari: तस्मात् स्वस्थेन मनसा विमृश्य गतिमात्मनः । द्वैते ध्रुवार्थविश्रम्भं त्यजोपशममाविश ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt svasthena manasā vimṛśya gatimātmanaḥ dvaite dhruvārthaviśrambhaṃ tyajopaśamamāviśa
translation:
Therefore, examining carefully the real nature of the Soul and with concentration of mind, give up the belief in the permanence of the objective world as it implies duality. Betake yourself to renunciation and serenity of the mind.
[Note: Additional verses in Padaratnāvalī’s text of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa:]
(1) A king named Bhoja dreamt that he subsisted on the remnant of the food (out of the dishes) of his enemy. He was therefore overcome with nausia, grief, delusion, fear and distress and became utterly miserable.
(2) (In the dream) some (of his enemies) who nursed age-old animosity and vengeance, carried away the objects he enjoyed; some killed his dependents; some abducted his wives while others beheaded him.
(3) Awaking from this dream overflooded with unending miseries, he reflected deeply over it. He got disgusted and renounced his house (worldly life).
(4) Externally appearing as insane, he visualized the Supreme Soul within his heart, free from attachments. Overcoming conflicting states of the mind he established it in the Supreme Brahman called Śrī Nārāyaṇa, untouched by the conditioning perishable body. In this absorbed state of meditation (in a-samprajñāta-samādhi), he totally forgot that he was conditioned by the body.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाचएतां मन्त्रोपनिषदं प्रतीच्छ प्रयतो मम । यां धारयन् सप्तरात्राद् द्रष्टा सङ्कर्षणं प्रभुम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācaetāṃ mantropaniṣadaṃ pratīccha prayato mama yāṃ dhārayan saptarātrād draṣṭā saṅkarṣaṇaṃ prabhum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative plural from √ pre class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ pre class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ pre class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation:
Nārada said:
After purifying yourself (i.e. after disposing of the dead body of the child and taking a bath thereafter) accept from me the Mantropaniṣad (Vide Infra Ch. XVI, 18-25). By bearing this in mind and meditating over it, you will visualize Lord, Saṅkarṣaṇa within seven nights.
